title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are incredibly easy to make using just two ingredients: lemons and sea salt. This traditional salt-cured fermentation method produces highly flavorful lemons with a bright citrus taste and umami depth from fermentation.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 3 to 4 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with generous amounts of salt, using all the salt until each lemon is filled.
Pack the whole lemons into a clean, large jar as tightly as possible, and lemon juice will be released in the process.
Once the jar is full, ensure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice, adding juice from the extra lemon if needed.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Once opened, store in the fridge indefinitely, rinsing off any excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes.
